instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
How Has ULIP Evolved Over the Years?
ULIP, or Unit-Linked Insurance Plans, are unique life insurance policies that allow you to invest in the financial markets. These plans direct your premium to different funds that you choose. Each of these funds invests in securities that are traded on the financial markets. As such, you can participate in market-linked returns and build up an attractive corpus for your financial goals.
The ULIPs of today have evolved with time ever since they were introduced in the early years of the 2000s. This evolution journey is fraught with considerable changes and innovations in the plan to make it what it is today. Let us have a look at how ULIPs, as a product, developed over the years.
ULIPs before 2010 – the launch of a new product
The Unit Trust of India (UTI) is credited for launching the first-ever ULIP in 19711. After that, many insurers adopted the concept and started offering different types of ULIPs to their policyholders. The ULIPs of that time, i.e., after their launch and till 2010, had the following features –
The premium allocation charges in the initial years were very high. Such charges ate into the premium paid for the first few years, and a low amount was invested in the chosen funds.
The lock-in period was three years.
Moreover, such ULIPs were highly missold as intermediaries asked their customers to invest for three years only and get attractive returns. As such, many policies got discontinued after three years bringing down the persistency ratio of insurers.
The next-gen ULIPs – between 2010 and 2015
The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) saw the rampant misselling of ULIPs and frequent policy discontinuations. To curb such malpractices and make ULIPs more customer-friendly, IRDAI made multiple changes to ULIPs. These changes include the following –
Increasing awareness about ULIP products by facilitating customer education programs.
Reducing the charges associated with ULIPs so that a larger proportion of the premium gets allocated to selected funds. As such, the charges were limited to 2.25% for the first 10 years of the policy.
The lock-in period in the ULIP plan was increased from three to five years.
The minimum sum assured was increased to 10 times the premium for adequate coverage
The evolution to 3G ULIPs – between 2015 and 2017
2015 saw the introduction of low-cost ULIPs wherein the charges were extremely low. Online plans were launched, eliminating the commission charges and bringing the cost down considerably. Some of the ULIP charges were capped at 1.45% to 1.50% by many companies to attract customers.
Modern-day ULIPs
As ULIPs gained popularity with the introduction of low charges and increased customer awareness, insurance companies started rolling out innovative products. Today, ULIPs have undergone tremendous changes compared to those that were available in the early 2000s. Some of the modern-day features and benefits of ULIPs are as follows –
ULIP charges are reduced and spread over the entire policy tenure.
Some plans even refund the mortality charges on maturity.
Many policies have eliminated the policy administration and premium allocation charges.
The fund management charges are also limited to 1.35% per annum.
Moreover, the Union Budget 2021 also changed the tax treatment of ULIPs. The changes rules are as follows 2–
If you buy the ULIP plan before 1st February 2021, the maturity benefit will be tax-free under Section 10(10D), provided the premium is within 10% of the sum assured.
If you buy the policy on or after 1st February 2021, the maturity benefit will be tax-free only if the aggregate premium for all ULIPs is up to Rs.2.5 lakhs. If the premium exceeds Rs.2.5 lakhs, the returns earned will be taxed.
The bottom line
Modern ULIPs are steps ahead of their previous versions. ULIPs have evolved to be more customer-centric so that you get the investment option. So, assess the ULIPs available in the market and choose a plan that matches your investment needs and financial goals. Invest in a suitable plan and enjoy the dual benefits of ULIPs in the form of investment returns and insurance protection.
Disclaimer:
In this policy, the investment risk in investment portfolio is borne by the policyholder. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
